docs.title: 前言
docs.text:
如果你也对计算机和艺术抱有双重热爱，对哪部分都无法割舍，那本书是为自己就业的自学指南。各公司的技术美术的岗位细分都不完全一样，但相信你在探索的过程中能找到自己热爱与价值的结合。 感谢 CS自学指南，从中收获了非常多，本书结构也参考此书：https://csdiy.wiki/

作为计算机和艺术跨专业的学生来说，最困难的就是对自己的定位-有非常多计算机的路径，也有非常多艺术的路径，但跨专业的路径非常少。这一部分是因为这两个学科单独本身就足够一个人投入全部精力去研究，更别说把两个专业都学好。两个专业都学的后果就是哪个都不精。幸运的是，在这个AI兴起的时代，入门一个专业的难度前所未有地低。你只要敢想敢执行，一切皆有可能。在未来的10年，或许我们能真正看到跨专业人才的兴起。

docs.title: 技术美术职业路径
docs.text: 技术美术大致分为动画、管线、

docs.title: 技术美术基础
docs.text:
Choose your path:

  • Core Skills - Essential skills for all Technical Artists
  • Pipeline Development - Focus on asset pipelines and workflow optimization
  • Shaders & Graphics - Specialize in real-time rendering and materials
  • Animation Systems - Master rigging and animation tools
  • Visual Effects - Create stunning real-time VFX
  • Tools Development - Build custom tools for artists

docs.title: Why Technical Art?
docs.text:
Technical Artists are crucial to modern game development because they:

  • Bridge the gap between art and engineering teams
  • Optimize art production workflows
  • Solve complex technical challenges
  • Ensure visual quality and performance
  • Enable artists to work efficiently

docs.title: How to Use This Guide
docs.text:
1. Start with the Introduction to understand the role
  2. Master the Core Skills
  3. Choose a specialization path
  4. Follow the learning resources and practice projects
  5. Join the technical art community

docs.title: Core Skills for Technical Artists
docs.text: This section covers the fundamental skills and knowledge that every Technical Artist should possess, regardless of their specialization.

docs.title: Learning Resources
docs.text:

  • 🎥 The Art of Code (Inigo Quilez)
  • 📄 Scratchapixel 2.0
  • 🎮 Learn OpenGL

docs.text:

  • Coordinate Systems
  • Rendering Pipeline
  • Lighting and Shading Models

  • 📚 Python for Artists (Jeremy Cantor)
  • 💻 Shader Development (Shader Toy)

  • 📚 Real-Time Rendering

docs.title: Recommended Learning Path
docs.text:
1. Start with programming basics (Python/C#)
  2. Learn one DCC tool thoroughly (Maya or Blender)
  3. Master fundamental 3D graphics concepts
  4. Get comfortable with one game engine
  5. Learn version control
  6. Explore specialized areas

docs.title: Practice Projects
docs.text:
1. Create a custom asset processing tool
  2. Build a shader library
  3. Develop an artist-friendly tool UI
  4. Set up an automated pipeline for asset conversion
  5. Create a performance profiling tool

Remember: Focus on understanding the underlying principles rather than just learning software. Tools change, but fundamentals remain constant.

docs.title: Preface
docs.text:
Self-Study Path for Technical Artists If you have a deep passion for both computer science and art and can’t bear to give up either, then this guide is meant to help you chart a self-learning path toward employment. The roles and responsibilities of technical artists vary across companies, but through your own exploration, you’ll be able to find the intersection of passion and value that suits you best. Thanks to the CS Self-Learning Guide, from which I’ve gained a lot of insight—this guide’s structure is also inspired by it: https://csdiy.wiki/

As a student coming from an interdisciplinary background in computer science and art, the hardest challenge is defining your identity. There are countless paths in the field of computer science, and just as many in art, but there are very few established paths that combine both. This is partly because each discipline on its own demands a full commitment of time and energy—let alone mastering both. As a result, trying to study both may lead to not excelling in either.

Fortunately, in this age of AI, it’s easier than ever to get started in a new field. If you have the courage to imagine and take action, anything is possible. In the next 10 years, we may truly witness the rise of interdisciplinary talents.

docs.title: What is a Technical Artist?
docs.text: A Technical Artist (TA) bridges the gap between artists and programmers in game development. They combine artistic vision with technical expertise to solve complex problems, optimize workflows, and ensure the seamless integration of art assets into game engines.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Choose your path:

  • Core Skills - Essential skills for all Technical Artists
  • Pipeline Development - Focus on asset pipelines and workflow optimization
  • Shaders & Graphics - Specialize in real-time rendering and materials
  • Animation Systems - Master rigging and animation tools
  • Visual Effects - Create stunning real-time VFX
  • Tools Development - Build custom tools for artists

docs.title: Why Technical Art?
docs.text:
Technical Artists are crucial to modern game development because they:

  • Bridge the gap between art and engineering teams
  • Optimize art production workflows
  • Solve complex technical challenges
  • Ensure visual quality and performance
  • Enable artists to work efficiently

docs.text:
• Build a portfolio focusing on technical solutions

Remember: - Focus on both artistic and technical skills - Learn to bridge art and engineering teams - Document your tools and systems - Stay current with game engine updates - Practice optimization techniques - Build a strong foundation in mathematics

  • Shader Community
  • Technical Artist Forum

docs.title: 1. Foundation (1-3 months)
docs.text:
• Learn basic particle systems
  • Understand shaders fundamentals
  • Master one game engine's VFX system
  • Study color theory and composition

docs.title: 2. Intermediate (3-6 months)
docs.text:
• Advanced particle systems
  • Custom shader development
  • Performance optimization
  • Tool development

docs.title: 3. Advanced (6+ months)
docs.text:
• Complex VFX systems
  • Advanced optimization
  • Pipeline development
  • Specialized effects

docs.title: Example Effects to Study
docs.text:
1. God of War - Weapon Effects
  2. Diablo IV - Spell Effects
  3. Control - Environmental Effects
  4. Doom Eternal - Gore System

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Start with reference gathering
  • Build modular systems
  • Document your work
  • Consider performance early
  • Test across different hardware
  • Maintain consistent style
  • Create reusable components

docs.title: Portfolio Development
docs.text:
• Document your process
  • Show before/after optimization
  • Include technical breakdowns
  • Demonstrate problem-solving
  • Show real-time implementations
  • Include performance metrics

Remember: - Focus on real-time performance - Build a strong technical foundation - Learn from existing games - Stay updated with new techniques - Network with other VFX artists - Document your learning process - Practice regularly with small projects
